reasoning: By October 2025, climate data covering most of the year consistently shows that 2025 is falling behind both 2024 and 2023 in global average temperatures. Dr. Gavin Schmidt (Director of NASA GISS) and major climate reporting groups (Carbon Brief, Berkeley Earth, WMO, and Copernicus/C3S) project 2025 will most likely be the third-warmest year on record. Berkeley Earth explicitly calculated a "next-to-nothing chance" of 2025 being a record-warm year as of August 2025 data. September was also measured as the third-warmest September. Given that 2025 trails the records set in 2024 by a significant margin for the first 9 months of the year, it is practically mathematically impossible for the final NASA GISTEMP annual average to surpass 2024. Therefore, the probability resolves to the near-zero lower bound.
